summary: I reviewed the iteration-2 technical design (`design-writer/output/design.md`) for the Human-in-the-Loop Pause feature against `guides/backend-guide.md` and `guides/ui-style-guide.md`, grounding each check against the actual repo (existing `_define_panel.html.erb`, `result.rb`, `phases_controller.rb`, the `app/queries/` tree, and Step's `active_run?`/`latest_run`/`worker_executed?`/`worker_predecessors` methods). All three findings from iteration 1 are resolved: the paused-menu buttons now use the guide's `px-3 py-2 text-sm font-semibold` sizing, every menu submit carries `data-turbo-submits-with` (Ask Human is an anchor, so correctly omits it), and `Phases::Convergence` is relocated to `app/queries/` and labeled a read-only query-object predicate. The rest of the design holds up: verb-first `self.call → Result` services with service-owned transactions and after-commit broadcasts, thin PRG controllers mirroring the existing `send_back`/`answers` actions, models limited to the `paused` enum plus a small predicate with no business-logic callbacks, a reversible migration, the `paused → amber "attention"` mapping the UI guide explicitly names, labels always paired with status badges, `aria-live` on progress messages, and Minitest coverage for every new unit. I recorded one non-blocking styling observation (the destructive "Repeat from the Beginning" button styled as a neutral secondary) that does not rise to a guide violation, and wrote a **pass** verdict with no findings to `verdict.json`.
